pet.name: Soe
pet.special_needs: Very playful, Need constant monitoring, Strict dietary, Bad Stomach
pet.characteristics: Golden Retrievers are highly social, energetic dogs requiring daily exercise, mental stimulation, and consistent training. They thrive on interaction and structured play to prevent behavioral issues. Regular grooming is essential due to their double coat. Routine veterinary care, including joint monitoring, supports long-term health. Feeding should follow a consistent schedule with high-quality nutrition; individual sensitivities may require dietary adjustment under veterinary guidance. These dogs are prone to ear infections and hip dysplasia, warranting preventive care and monitoring. They respond well to positive reinforcement and benefit from early socialization.
pet.species_profile_summary: Golden Retrievers typically live 10-12 years and require active engagement, consistent care routines, and attentive health monitoring throughout their lifespan.

age_context.summary: Golden Retrievers typically live 10-12 years and require active engagement, consistent care routines, and attentive health monitoring throughout their lifespan.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Medication Routine and Safety
retrieved_passages.content: Medication-related tasks in a home routine should focus on reminders, documentation, and observation, not dosage changes. A planning assistant can remind the owner to give medication exactly as already prescribed and to log whether the dose was completed.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

retrieved_passages.title: Puppy and Kitten Routine Basics
retrieved_passages.content: Exercise and enrichment for very young pets should stay short and positive. Puppies often do better with several brief walks or training moments instead of one long outing, and kittens benefit from short interactive play sessions that stop before exhaustion.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning meal and hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Feed Soe at consistent time following veterinary plan; monitor water intake and appetite.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Strict dietary needs and sensitive stomach require consistent feeding schedule and hydration monitoring.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening meal and digestion monitoring
raw_recommendations.notes: Feed second meal at consistent time; observe for any digestive upset or refusal.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Bad stomach requires consistent feeding times and close observation for changes.

raw_recommendations.name: Active play and mental enrichment
raw_recommendations.notes: Multiple short play sessions with toys, fetch, or interactive games to match high energy and playfulness.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Golden Retrievers are highly energetic and playful; structured play prevents behavioral issues and supports mental stimulation.

raw_recommendations.name: Sniff walk and bathroom breaks
raw_recommendations.notes: Gentle walk with sniffing opportunities; monitor bathroom habits for digestive changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Daily exercise supports health; monitoring bathroom habits helps track stomach sensitivity.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening calm interaction and sleep prep
raw_recommendations.notes: Gentle brushing, calm play, or quiet time before bedtime to establish consistent sleep routine.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent sleep schedule supports digestive health and overall well-being for a 6-year-old Golden.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly grooming and coat check
raw_recommendations.notes: Brush double coat to prevent matting; check ears for signs of infection.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Golden Retrievers require regular grooming; ear monitoring helps prevent infections.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly health observation log
raw_recommendations.notes: Document appetite, energy, gait, bathroom habits, and any digestive changes; report major changes to veterinarian.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Proactive monitoring of age-related changes and stomach sensitivity supports early detection of issues.
